message.content:
I need to build endpoint-to-scope mappings for Slack and Discord APIs. For each, I need a JSON file mapping REST API endpoints to OAuth scopes sufficient to authorize them. This is for a proxy-based policy enforcement system.

Output format for each:
```json
{
  "provider": "slack",
  "apiHost": "slack.com",
  "basePath": "/api",
  "scopeMap": [
    {
      "method": "GET|POST",
      "pathPattern": "/api/chat.postMessage",
      "sufficientScopes": ["chat:write"],
      "description": "Send a message"
    }
  ],
  "allScopes": ["chat:write", ...]
}
```

## 1. Slack (slack.com/api)
Slack API methods are all POST to `/api/{method_name}`. Slack has granular OAuth scopes.

Key Slack scopes include: channels:read, channels:write, channels:history, channels:join, channels:manage, chat:write, chat:write.public, chat:write.customize, conversations:read, conversations:write, conversations:history, conversations:connect:read, conversations:connect:write, conversations:connect:manage, dnd:read, dnd:write, emoji:read, files:read, files:write, groups:read, groups:write, groups:history, im:read, im:write, im:history, links:read, links:write, mpim:read, mpim:write, mpim:history, pins:read, pins:write, reactions:read, reactions:write, reminders:read, reminders:write, search:read, stars:read, stars:write, team:read, team.billing:read, team.preferences:read, usergroups:read, usergroups:write, users:read, users:read.email, users:write, users.profile:read, users.profile:write, bookmarks:read, bookmarks:write, calls:read, calls:write, metadata.message:read

Cover ALL major Slack Web API methods including:
- chat.postMessage, chat.update, chat.delete, chat.scheduleMessage, chat.meMessage
- conversations.list, conversations.info, conversations.history, conversations.members, conversations.create, conversations.invite, conversations.kick, conversations.join, conversations.leave, conversations.archive, conversations.unarchive, conversations.rename, conversations.setPurpose, conversations.setTopic, conversations.open, conversations.close, conversations.replies
- channels.* (legacy but still used)
- users.list, users.info, users.lookupByEmail, users.setPresence, users.profile.get, users.profile.set
- files.upload, files.list, files.info, files.delete, files.sharedPublicURL, files.revokePublicURL
- reactions.add, reactions.remove, reactions.list, reactions.get
- pins.add, pins.remove, pins.list
- stars.add, stars.remove, stars.list
- search.messages, search.files, search.all
- reminders.add, reminders.delete, reminders.list, reminders.complete, reminders.info
- bookmarks.add, bookmarks.edit, bookmarks.list, bookmarks.remove
- team.info, team.accessLogs, team.billableInfo
- usergroups.create, usergroups.disable, usergroups.enable, usergroups.list, usergroups.update, usergroups.users.list, usergroups.users.update
- emoji.list
- dnd.endDnd, dnd.endSnooze, dnd.info, dnd.setSnooze, dnd.teamInfo
- views.open, views.push, views.update, views.publish

All Slack API calls are POST requests to /api/{method}.

Search the web for "Slack API methods scopes reference" to find the complete list.

## 2. Discord (discord.com/api/v10)
Discord OAuth2 scopes: identify, email, connections, guilds, guilds.join, guilds.members.read, gdm.join, bot, messages.read, webhook.incoming, applications.commands, applications.builds.read, applications.builds.upload, activities.read, activities.write, relationships.read, role_connections.write, voice

For Discord bot API endpoints, map them to these scopes based on what they access. Discord uses bot tokens, but we'll map endpoints to semantic scopes for policy enforcement.

Key areas:
- Guilds (get, create, modify, delete)
- Channels (get, create, modify, delete, messages)
- Messages (get, create, edit, delete, bulk delete, reactions, pins)
- Members (get, list, add, modify, kick, ban)
- Roles (get, create, modify, delete)
- Webhooks
- Voice
- Invites (get, create, delete)
- Users (get current, modify)
- Interactions / commands
- Audit logs

Use path patterns like: /api/v10/guilds/*/channels, /api/v10/channels/*/messages

Use semantic scopes like: guilds.read, guilds.write, messages.read, messages.write, members.read, members.write, channels.read, channels.write, roles.read, roles.write, bans.read, bans.write, webhooks.read, webhooks.write, invites.read, invites.write, voice.read, voice.write, audit_log.read

Write:
- /workspace/scope-maps/slack.json
- /workspace/scope-maps/discord.json

Be thorough. Search the web for the complete list of endpoints and their scope requirements.

1. **Scopes Reference** — Slack's developer docs provide a list of OAuth scopes and the permissions they grant to your Slack app. You can find this at [docs.slack.dev/reference/scopes](https://docs.slack.dev/reference/scopes/).

### How Scopes Work with API Methods

- For Conversations API methods, the required scopes depend on the type of channel-like object you're working with — you only need the scopes corresponding to that conversation type.

- The types of channels you interface with in the Conversations API are governed by corresponding permission scopes. For example, to retrieve details about a public channel, you'll need the `channels:read` scope. For details about a private channel, you'll need the `groups:read` scope.

- `conversations.list` accepts `channels:read`, `groups:read`, `mpim:read`, and `im:read`. If you only have `channels:read`, then `conversations.list` will only return public channels. If you have both `channels:read` and `im:read`, then methods will only return public channels and DMs.

- OAuth scopes let you specify exactly how your app needs to access a Slack user's account. As an app developer, you specify your desired scopes in the initial OAuth authorization request, and when a user responds, the requested scopes will be displayed to them for approval.

- There is a planned sunset date of 2025-11-12 for the `files.upload` method. Migrating when possible is recommended, and `filesUploadV2` offers advantages.

### Common Scopes Examples

- Need to post messages? Request the `chat:write` scope.
- Common scopes include: `channels:read`, `chat:write`, `commands`, `groups:read`, `mpim:read`, `im:read`, and many more.

Here's a comprehensive overview of Discord's REST API v10 endpoints, OAuth2 scopes, and bot permissions based on the latest documentation:

## Discord REST API v10 — OAuth2, Scopes & Bot Permissions

### API Base URL
The Discord API v10 base endpoint is `https://discord.com/api/v10`.

### OAuth2 Overview
OAuth2 enables application developers to build applications that utilize authentication and data from the Discord API. Within Discord, there are multiple types of OAuth2 authentication. Discord supports the authorization code grant, the implicit grant, client credentials, and some modified special-for-Discord flows for Bots and Webhooks.

### Authentication & Token Types
Discord's API supports three distinct token types, each serving different authentication purposes. All tokens are submitted via the Authorization HTTP header, but with different prefixes and use cases.

- **User Tokens:** User tokens represent authentication for a Discord user account. They provide full access to all API routes available to that user, including user-specific endpoints and operations that require user context.
- **Bot Tokens:** Bot tokens are obtained from the app's Bot page in the Developer Portal and provide full bot permissions within installed servers. OAuth2 bearer tokens are acquired through the authorization flow and grant access based on approved scopes (like `identify`, `guilds`, `email`).
- **OAuth2 Bearer Tokens:** OAuth2 bearer tokens provide scoped access to the API on behalf of a user. These tokens are obtained through one of the OAuth2 grant flows and restrict access to only the scopes the user has authorized.

### OAuth2 Token Endpoints

1. **Token Exchange** (`POST /oauth2/token`): Used to exchange an authorization code for an access token. The access token allows your application to make certain requests to the API on their behalf, restricted to whatever scopes were requested.
2. **Token Refresh**: The `expires_in` field indicates how long, in seconds, until the returned access token expires, allowing you to anticipate the expiration and refresh the token.
3. **Token Revocation** (`POST /oauth2/token/revoke`): To disable an access or refresh token, you can revoke it by making a POST request to the token revocation URL. When you revoke a token, any active access or refresh tokens associated with that authorization will be revoked.

### OAuth2 Scopes
Discord supports a list of all OAuth2 scopes. Some scopes require approval from Discord to use. Requesting them from a user without approval from Discord may cause errors or undocumented behavior in the OAuth2 flow.

Key scopes include: `identify`, `email`, `guilds`, `connections`, `bot`, `applications.commands`, `applications.commands.update`, `guilds.join`, `role_connections.write`, `gateway.connect`, and more.

- Team applications are limited to the `identify` and `applications.commands.update` scope, because teams are not bound to a specific user.
- `role_connections.write` cannot be used with the Implicit grant type.
- OAuth2 bearer tokens can only connect to the Gateway if the `gateway.connect` scope is authorized.

### Bot Authorization & Permissions
Bot authorization is a special server-less and callback-less OAuth2 flow that makes it easy for users to add bots to guilds. The URL looks like: `https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=...&scope=bot&permissions=1`. There's also a `permissions` parameter, which is an integer corresponding to the permission calculations for the bot.

Bot scopes define the level of access your bot has to user data and API endpoints. They are an essential part of OAuth2 authentication and determine what information and actions are available to your bot when interacting with the Discord API.

### Privileged Gateway Intents (2025 Update)
Guild Members, Guild Presences, and Message Content are privileged intents. Understanding Discord bot permissions, OAuth scopes, and privileged intents is essential for safe, least-privilege setups.

- You must explicitly enable them in the Developer Portal. If your bot serves 100+ guilds, Discord may require additional verification and justification for continued access. Expect to document why you need each privileged intent, how you use it, and how you minimize data retention.
- Message Content: Only request when you truly parse raw message text. Prefer application commands and context menus otherwise.

### 2025–2026 Changes
- As of 2025-04-03, file upload limits are enforced per-attachment rather than per-message. The file upload size limit applies to each file in a request, with a default limit of 10 MiB for all users (higher for Nitro users or boosted servers).
- The latest guides cover root causes of permission issues, step-by-step fixes, latest 2026 permission changes, and modern best practices for bot development.

### Security Best Practices
- When implementing OAuth2 flows, always use the state parameter to prevent CSRF attacks. The state should be a cryptographically random value that binds the authorization request to the user's session.
- Tokens are highly sensitive credentials. Never commit them to version control or expose them publicly. Store them in environment variables (.env files).
- Use the OAuth2 URL Generator in the Developer Portal to build invite links with specific permission bits and scopes. Validate the requested permissions with a calculator before sharing the invite.

Here is a comprehensive reference of Slack API OAuth permission scopes relevant to **chat**, **conversations**, **users**, **files**, **reactions**, and **pins**:

---

## Slack API Permission Scopes Reference

### How Scopes Work

Slack's system of OAuth permission scopes governs usage of Slack apps and their use of the Web API, Events API, RTM API, Slash Commands, and incoming webhooks. Slack uses scopes that refer to the object they grant access to, followed by the class of actions on that object they allow (e.g. `file:write`). Some scopes have an optional perspective which is either user, bot, or admin, which influences how the action appears in Slack (e.g. `chat:write:user` will send a message from the authorizing user as opposed to your app).

There are two types of scopes as part of the new granular permission system: **Bot Token Scopes** govern the information that an app can access directly as a bot; **User Token Scopes** govern the app's ability to access user data and act on behalf of the specific user that has authorized it.

---

### Chat Scopes

| Scope | Description | Token Types |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| `chat:write` | Post messages in approved channels & conversations | User, Bot |
| `chat:write.customize` | Send messages as @your_slack_app with a customized username and avatar | Legacy bot, Bot |
| `chat:write.public` | Send messages to channels @your_slack_app isn't a member of | Legacy bot, Bot |

For Slack apps, `chat:write` replaces both `chat:write:user` and `chat:write:bot`.

The `chat.postMessage` API method requires the `chat:write` scope.

---

### Conversations Scopes

The Conversations API allows you to review history, create or archive channels, invite team members, set conversation topics and purpose — and the types of channels you interface with are governed by corresponding permission scopes.

| Scope | Description | Token Types |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| `channels:read` | View basic info about public channels | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `channels:history` | View messages and other content in public channels that your slack app has been added to | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `channels:manage` | Manage public channels the app has been added to | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `channels:join` | Join incident or announcement channels | Bot |
| `groups:read` | View basic information about private channels that your slack app has been added to | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `groups:history` | View messages and other content in private channels that your slack app has been added to | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `groups:write` | Manage private channels that your slack app has been added to and create new ones | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `im:history` | View messages and other content in direct messages that your slack app has been added to | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `im:read` | View basic info about DMs | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `im:write` | Start DMs | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `mpim:read` | View basic info about group DMs | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `mpim:history` | View messages in multi-party DMs | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `mpim:write` | Start group DMs | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `conversations:history` | Deprecated: Retrieve conversation history for legacy workspace apps | — |
| `conversations:read` | Deprecated: Retrieve information on conversations for legacy workspace apps | — |
| `conversations:write` | Deprecated: Edit conversation attributes for legacy workspace apps | — |
| `conversations:write.invites` | Invite members to conversations | — |

`conversations.list` accepts `channels:read`, `groups:read`, `mpim:read`, and `im:read`. If you only have `channels:read`, then `conversations.list` will only return public channels. If you have both `channels:read` and `im:read`, then methods will only return public channels and DMs, and so on.

---

### Users Scopes

| Scope | Description | Token Types |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| `users:read` | View people in a workspace | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `users:read.email` | View email addresses of people in a workspace | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `users:write` | Set presence for your app | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `users.profile:read` | View profile details | User, Bot |
| `users.profile:write` | Edit a user's profile info and status | User |

The `users:read.email` OAuth scope is now required to access the email field in user objects returned by the `users.list` and `users.info` web API methods. `users:read` is no longer a sufficient scope for this data field.

Apps created after January 4th, 2017 must request both the `users:read` and `users:read.email` OAuth permission scopes simultaneously when using the OAuth app installation flow to enable access to the email field.

---

### Files Scopes

| Scope | Description | Token Types |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| `files:read` | View files shared in channels and conversations that your slack app has been added to | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `files:write` | Upload, edit, and delete files as your slack app | User, Bot |
| `files:write:user` | Upload, edit, and delete files as your slack app (legacy) | User, Legacy bot |
| `remote_files:read` | View remote files added by the app in a workspace | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `remote_files:write` | Add, edit, and delete remote files | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `remote_files:share` | Share remote files | User, Legacy bot, Bot |

`files:read` used to be sufficient to read file comments, but as comments became messages in threads, your app must be awarded a conversations "history" scope like `channels:history`. Similarly, `files:write` was once adequate to comment on a file, but now your app must be awarded a `chat:write` scope to comment on files.

---

### Reactions Scopes

| Scope | Description | Token Types |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| `reactions:read` | View emoji reactions and their associated content in channels and conversations that your slack app has been added to | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `reactions:write` | Add and edit emoji reactions | User, Legacy bot, Bot |

If you've configured your subscription to receive `reaction_added`, `reaction_removed`, and `file_created` events, you won't receive all three unless you request the `reactions:read` and `files:read` scopes from the user.

---

### Pins Scopes

| Scope | Description | Token Types |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| `pins:read` | View pinned content in channels and conversations that your slack app has been added to | User, Legacy bot, Bot |
| `pins:write` | Add and remove pinned messages and files | User, Legacy bot, Bot |

---

### How to Check Scopes at Runtime

When calling the Slack API, you can check the HTTP headers to see what scopes you have — `x-oauth-scopes` lists the scopes your token has authorized, and `x-accepted-oAuth-scopes` lists the scopes that the action checks for.

### Requesting Multiple Scopes

When making the initial authorization request, your application can request multiple scopes as a space or comma separated list (e.g. `teams:read users:read`).

---

### Key Official References

- **Permission scopes list**: [api.slack.com/scopes](https://api.slack.com/scopes)
- **Web API methods**: [api.slack.com/methods](https://api.slack.com/methods)
- **Scopes reference (new docs)**: [docs.slack.dev/reference/scopes](https://docs.slack.dev/reference/scopes/)
- **Conversations API guide**: [docs.slack.dev/apis/web-api/using-the-conversations-api](https://docs.slack.dev/apis/web-api/using-the-conversations-api/)

# Discord Guild REST API Endpoints

## Guild Management
- **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}` - Retrieve guild information
- **PATCH** `/guilds/{guild.id}` - Update guild settings
- **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/preview` - Get public guild preview

## Channels
- **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/channels` - List all guild channels
- **POST** `/guilds/{guild.id}/channels` - Create new channel
- **PATCH** `/guilds/{guild.id}/channels` - Reorder channels
- **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/threads/active` - List active threads

## Members
- **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/members/{user.id}` - Get specific member
- **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/members` - List guild members
- **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/members/search` - Search members by username
- **PUT** `/guilds/{guild.id}/members/{user.id}` - Add member to guild
- **PATCH** `/guilds/{guild.id}/members/{user.id}` - Modify member attributes
- **PATCH** `/guilds/{guild.id}/members/@me` - Modify current user in guild
- **DELETE** `/guilds/{guild.id}/members/{user.id}` - Remove member

## Roles
- **PUT** `/guilds/{guild.id}/members/{user.id}/roles/{role.id}` - Add role to member
- **DELETE** `/guilds/{guild.id}/members/{user.id}/roles/{role.id}` - Remove role from member

## Bans
- **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/bans` - List banned users
- **PUT** `/guilds/{guild.id}/bans/{user.id}` - Ban user
- **DELETE** `/guilds/{guild.id}/bans/{user.id}` - Unban user

## Other Features
- **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/widget` - Retrieve widget settings
- **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/vanity-url` - Get vanity URL code
- **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/welcome-screen` - Fetch welcome screen
- **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/audit-logs` - Access audit log entries
- **POST** `/guilds/{guild.id}/prune` - Begin member pruning

# Discord User REST API Endpoints

| HTTP Method | URL Path | Description |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/users/@me` | "Returns the user object of the requester's account" |
| GET | `/users/{user.id}` | Retrieves a user profile for a specific user ID |
| PATCH | `/users/@me` | Modifies the authenticated user's account settings |
| GET | `/users/@me/guilds` | "Returns a list of partial guild objects the current user is a member of" |
| GET | `/users/@me/guilds/{guild.id}/member` | Retrieves guild membership details for the authenticated user |
| DELETE | `/users/@me/guilds/{guild.id}` | Allows a user to leave a guild |
| POST | `/users/@me/channels` | Creates a direct message channel with another user |
| POST | `/users/@me/channels` | Creates a group DM channel with multiple users |
| GET | `/users/@me/connections` | "Returns a list of connection objects" linked to the user |
| GET | `/users/@me/applications/{application.id}/role-connection` | Retrieves the application role connection for the user |
| PUT | `/users/@me/applications/{application.id}/role-connection` | Updates the application role connection for the user |

# Discord Channels REST API Endpoints

## Channel Management
- **GET** `/channels/{channel.id}` - Retrieve channel details
- **PATCH** `/channels/{channel.id}` - Update channel settings
- **DELETE** `/channels/{channel.id}` - Remove or close a channel

## Permissions
- **PUT** `/channels/{channel.id}/permissions/{overwrite.id}` - Set permission overwrites for users/roles
- **DELETE** `/channels/{channel.id}/permissions/{overwrite.id}` - Remove permission overwrites

## Invitations
- **GET** `/channels/{channel.id}/invites` - List active invites for a channel
- **POST** `/channels/{channel.id}/invites` - Generate new invite link

## Announcements
- **POST** `/channels/{channel.id}/followers` - Subscribe announcement channel to target

## User Interaction
- **POST** `/channels/{channel.id}/typing` - Display "user is typing" indicator
- **PUT** `/channels/{channel.id}/recipients/{user.id}` - Add member to group DM
- **DELETE** `/channels/{channel.id}/recipients/{user.id}` - Remove member from group DM

## Threads
- **POST** `/channels/{channel.id}/messages/{message.id}/threads` - Create thread from existing message
- **POST** `/channels/{channel.id}/threads` - Create standalone thread or forum post
- **PUT** `/channels/{channel.id}/thread-members/@me` - Join thread
- **PUT** `/channels/{channel.id}/thread-members/{user.id}` - Add member to thread
- **DELETE** `/channels/{channel.id}/thread-members/@me` - Leave thread
- **DELETE** `/channels/{channel.id}/thread-members/{user.id}` - Remove member from thread
- **GET** `/channels/{channel.id}/thread-members/{user.id}` - Get thread member details
- **GET** `/channels/{channel.id}/thread-members` - List thread members
- **GET** `/channels/{channel.id}/threads/archived/public` - List public archived threads
- **GET** `/channels/{channel.id}/threads/archived/private` - List private archived threads
- **GET** `/channels/{channel.id}/users/@me/threads/archived/private` - List joined private archived threads

# Discord Message REST API Endpoints

## Message Management

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| **GET** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages` | Retrieve messages in a channel, newest to oldest |
| **GET** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages/{message.id}` | Fetch a specific message |
| **POST** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages` | Send a new message to a channel |
| **PATCH** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages/{message.id}` | Modify an existing message's content or components |
| **DELETE** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages/{message.id}` | Remove a single message |
| **POST** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages/bulk-delete` | Remove multiple messages (2-100) in one request |

## Message Sharing

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| **POST** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages/{message.id}/crosspost` | Republish message to subscribed announcement channels |

## Reactions

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| **PUT** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages/{message.id}/reactions/{emoji.id}/@me` | Add your reaction to a message |
| **DELETE** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages/{message.id}/reactions/{emoji.id}/@me` | Remove your reaction |
| **DELETE** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages/{message.id}/reactions/{emoji.id}/{user.id}` | Remove another user's reaction |
| **GET** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages/{message.id}/reactions/{emoji.id}` | List users who reacted with an emoji |
| **DELETE** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages/{message.id}/reactions` | Clear all reactions from a message |
| **DELETE** | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages/{message.id}/reactions/{emoji.id}` | Remove all reactions for a specific emoji |

# Discord Invite REST API Endpoints

| HTTP Method | URL Path | Description |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/invites/{invite.code}` | "Returns an invite object for the given code" with optional member count and event data |
| DELETE | `/invites/{invite.code}` | Remove an invite; requires channel or guild management permissions |
| GET | `/invites/{invite.code}/target-users` | Retrieve CSV file listing user IDs permitted to accept the invite |
| PUT | `/invites/{invite.code}/target-users` | "Updates the users allowed to see and accept this invite" via CSV upload |
| GET | `/invites/{invite.code}/target-users/job-status` | Check processing status of asynchronous CSV target user operations |

# Discord Webhook REST API Endpoints

| HTTP Method | URL Path | Description |
|---|---|---|
| POST | `/channels/{channel.id}/webhooks` | Creates a new webhook |
| GET | `/channels/{channel.id}/webhooks` | Returns channel webhook objects |
| GET | `/guilds/{guild.id}/webhooks` | Returns guild webhook objects |
| GET | `/webhooks/{webhook.id}` | Retrieves a specific webhook |
| GET | `/webhooks/{webhook.id}/{webhook.token}` | Gets webhook without authentication |
| PATCH | `/webhooks/{webhook.id}` | Modifies webhook properties |
| PATCH | `/webhooks/{webhook.id}/{webhook.token}` | Updates webhook without authentication |
| DELETE | `/webhooks/{webhook.id}` | Permanently deletes a webhook |
| DELETE | `/webhooks/{webhook.id}/{webhook.token}` | Removes webhook without authentication |
| POST | `/webhooks/{webhook.id}/{webhook.token}` | Executes webhook to send messages |
| POST | `/webhooks/{webhook.id}/{webhook.token}/slack` | Sends Slack-compatible formatted messages |
| POST | `/webhooks/{webhook.id}/{webhook.token}/github` | Sends GitHub-compatible formatted messages |
| GET | `/webhooks/{webhook.id}/{webhook.token}/messages/{message.id}` | Retrieves previously-sent webhook message |
| PATCH | `/webhooks/{webhook.id}/{webhook.token}/messages/{message.id}` | Edits a webhook message |
| DELETE | `/webhooks/{webhook.id}/{webhook.token}/messages/{message.id}` | Deletes a webhook message |

# Discord Voice REST API Endpoints

Based on the documentation provided, here are all the voice-related REST API endpoints:

| HTTP Method | URL Path | Description |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/voice/regions` | "Returns an array of voice region objects that can be used when setting a voice or stage channel's rtc_region" |
| GET | `/guilds/{guild.id}/voice-states/@me` | Retrieves the current user's voice connection status within a guild |
| GET | `/guilds/{guild.id}/voice-states/{user.id}` | Retrieves a specific user's voice connection status within a guild |
| PATCH | `/guilds/{guild.id}/voice-states/@me` | Updates the current user's voice state (channel assignment, suppress status, or speak requests) |
| PATCH | `/guilds/{guild.id}/voice-states/{user.id}` | Updates another user's voice state (channel assignment or suppress status) |

**Note:** The PATCH endpoints return `204 No Content` on success and trigger a Voice State Update gateway event.

content.content:
# Discord Audit Logs REST API Endpoints

Based on the documentation provided, there is **one primary REST API endpoint** for audit logs:

## Get Guild Audit Log

**HTTP Method:** `GET`

**URL Path:** `/guilds/{guild.id}/audit-logs`

**Description:** Retrieves an audit log object for a specific guild. This endpoint requires the `VIEW_AUDIT_LOG` permission and returns a list of audit log entries ordered by ID. The endpoint supports optional query parameters to filter results by user ID, action type, and pagination using `before`, `after`, and `limit` parameters. The maximum number of entries returned is 100, with a default of 50.

---

The documentation does not list additional REST endpoints for audit logs beyond this single GET endpoint. All audit log interactions flow through this one resource path.

# Stage Instance REST API Endpoints

| HTTP Method | URL Path | Description |
|---|---|---|
| POST | `/stage-instances` | "Creates a new Stage instance associated to a Stage channel" and returns the created instance |
| GET | `/stage-instances/{channel.id}` | Retrieves the stage instance for a specified Stage channel if one exists |
| PATCH | `/stage-instances/{channel.id}` | "Updates fields of an existing Stage instance" and returns the modified instance |
| DELETE | `/stage-instances/{channel.id}` | Removes a Stage instance and returns a 204 No Content response |

# Guild Scheduled Events REST API Endpoints

| HTTP Method | URL Path | Description |
|-------------|----------|-------------|
| GET | `/guilds/{guild.id}/scheduled-events` | "Returns a list of guild scheduled event objects for the given guild." |
| POST | `/guilds/{guild.id}/scheduled-events` | "Create a guild scheduled event in the guild." Returns the created event object. |
| GET | `/guilds/{guild.id}/scheduled-events/{guild_scheduled_event.id}` | Retrieves a single scheduled event by its ID. |
| PATCH | `/guilds/{guild.id}/scheduled-events/{guild_scheduled_event.id}` | "Modify a guild scheduled event." Supports status updates and field changes. |
| DELETE | `/guilds/{guild.id}/scheduled-events/{guild_scheduled_event.id}` | Removes a scheduled event, returning a 204 status on success. |
| GET | `/guilds/{guild.id}/scheduled-events/{guild_scheduled_event.id}/users` | "Get a list of guild scheduled event users subscribed to a guild scheduled event." |

# Auto Moderation REST API Endpoints

| HTTP Method | URL Path | Description |
|---|---|---|
| **GET** | `/guilds/{guild.id}/auto-moderation/rules` | "Get a list of all rules currently configured for the guild" |
| **GET** | `/guilds/{guild.id}/auto-moderation/rules/{auto_moderation_rule.id}` | Retrieve a single moderation rule by its ID |
| **POST** | `/guilds/{guild.id}/auto-moderation/rules` | "Create a new rule" that automatically executes actions when triggered |
| **PATCH** | `/guilds/{guild.id}/auto-moderation/rules/{auto_moderation_rule.id}` | Update an existing moderation rule's configuration |
| **DELETE** | `/guilds/{guild.id}/auto-moderation/rules/{auto_moderation_rule.id}` | Remove a moderation rule from the guild |

All endpoints require the `MANAGE_GUILD` permission. The POST and PATCH operations support audit log reasons via the `X-Audit-Log-Reason` header.

# Discord Sticker REST API Endpoints

| HTTP Method | URL Path | Description |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/stickers/{sticker.id}` | "Returns a sticker object for the given sticker ID" |
| GET | `/sticker-packs` | "Returns a list of available sticker packs" |
| GET | `/sticker-packs/{pack.id}` | "Returns a sticker pack object for the given sticker pack ID" |
| GET | `/guilds/{guild.id}/stickers` | "Returns an array of sticker objects for the given guild" |
| GET | `/guilds/{guild.id}/stickers/{sticker.id}` | "Returns a sticker object for the given guild and sticker IDs" |
| POST | `/guilds/{guild.id}/stickers` | "Create a new sticker for the guild; requires `CREATE_GUILD_EXPRESSIONS`" |
| PATCH | `/guilds/{guild.id}/stickers/{sticker.id}` | "Modify the given sticker; requires appropriate expression management permissions" |
| DELETE | `/guilds/{guild.id}/stickers/{sticker.id}` | "Delete the given sticker; requires appropriate expression management permissions" |

## Application Endpoints

Here is a comprehensive summary of the **Discord REST API Guild Roles Endpoints** based on the official Discord developer documentation:

---

## Discord REST API — Guild Roles Endpoints

All endpoints are under the base path `/guilds/{guild.id}/roles`. Source: [Discord Developer Documentation](https://discord.com/developers/docs/resources/guild)

### 1. **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/roles` — List Guild Roles
Returns a list of role objects for the guild.

### 2. **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/roles/{role.id}` — Get Guild Role
Returns a role object for the specified role.

### 3. **GET** `/guilds/{guild.id}/roles/member-counts` — Get Guild Role Member Counts
Returns a map of role IDs to the number of members with the role. Does not include the @everyone role.

### 4. **POST** `/guilds/{guild.id}/roles` — Create Guild Role
Create a new role for the guild. Requires the MANAGE_ROLES permission. Returns the new role object on success. Fires a Guild Role Create Gateway event. All JSON params are optional. This endpoint supports the X-Audit-Log-Reason header.

### 5. **PATCH** `/guilds/{guild.id}/roles` — Modify Guild Role Positions
Modify the positions of a set of role objects for the guild. Requires the MANAGE_ROLES permission. Returns a list of all of the guild's role objects on success. Fires multiple Guild Role Update Gateway events. This endpoint supports the X-Audit-Log-Reason header.

### 6. **PATCH** `/guilds/{guild.id}/roles/{role.id}` — Modify Guild Role
Modify a guild role. Requires the MANAGE_ROLES permission. Returns the updated role on success. Fires a Guild Role Update Gateway event. All parameters to this endpoint are optional and nullable. This endpoint supports the X-Audit-Log-Reason header.

### 7. **DELETE** `/guilds/{guild.id}/roles/{role.id}` — Delete Guild Role
Delete a guild role. Requires the MANAGE_ROLES permission. Returns a 204 empty response on success. Fires a Guild Role Delete Gateway event. This endpoint supports the X-Audit-Log-Reason header.

---

### Related Member-Role Endpoints

- **PUT** `/guilds/{guild.id}/members/{user.id}/roles/{role.id}` — Add Guild Member Role
  Adds a role to a guild member. Requires the MANAGE_ROLES permission. Returns a 204 empty response on success.

---

### Notes
- The `color` field will still be returned by the API, but using the `colors` field is recommended when doing requests.
- Currently, endpoints like `GET /guilds/:guildId/roles/member-counts` and `GET /guilds/:guildId/roles/:roleId/member-ids` are available for users only. If a bot tries to access the `/member-ids` endpoint, it returns a 403 with the message "Bots cannot use this endpoint".

For full details, see the official documentation at: https://discord.com/developers/docs/resources/guild

Here is a summary of the required bot token scopes for the three Slack Conversations API methods:

---

## Slack API: Required Bot Token Scopes

### 1. `conversations.create`

This method creates a public or private channel using the Conversations API.

**Required Bot Token Scopes:**
- `channels:manage` for public channels, or `groups:write` for private ones.
- The `conversations.create` API method needs the scope `channels:manage` (for public channels).

### 2. `conversations.invite`

This Conversations API method invites 1-1000 users to a public or private channel. The calling user must be a member of the channel.

**Required Bot Token Scopes:**
- `channels:manage` for inviting to **public** channels
- `groups:write` for inviting to **private** channels
- There are also more granular scopes available: `channels:write.invites` to invite members to public channels, and `groups:write.invites` to invite members to private channels.

The `groups:write` scope confirms it covers `conversations.invite`: the `groups:write` scope allows managing private channels and grants access to `conversations.archive`, `conversations.create`, `conversations.invite` and other conversation methods.

### 3. `conversations.archive`

This method archives a conversation. Not all types of conversations can be archived.

**Required Bot Token Scopes:**
- `channels:manage` for archiving **public** channels
- `groups:write` for archiving **private** channels

This is confirmed by third-party documentation: `channels:write` is needed to create and archive public incident channels, and `groups:write` to create and archive private incident channels.

---

### Summary Table

| Method | Public Channels | Private Channels | Granular Alternative |
|---|---|---|---|
| `conversations.create` | `channels:manage` | `groups:write` | — |
| `conversations.invite` | `channels:manage` | `groups:write` | `channels:write.invites` / `groups:write.invites` |
| `conversations.archive` | `channels:manage` | `groups:write` | — |

### Key Notes

- The types of channels you interface with in the Conversations API are governed by corresponding permission scopes. For example, to retrieve details about a public channel, you'll need the `channels:read` scope. For details about a private channel, you'll need the `groups:read` scope.
- All Conversations API endpoints still accept multiple scopes and filter access to channels based on the provided token's scope.
- When using bot tokens, you need `channels:read` and `channels:manage` for public channels, and `groups:read` and `groups:write` for private channels. If you encounter `missing_scope` errors, check the scopes against the official Slack documentation.

Here's a summary of the **Discord REST API Application Commands Endpoints** based on the official Discord documentation:

## Global Application Commands

These endpoints manage commands available across all guilds where your app is installed.

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| **GET** | `/applications/{application.id}/commands` | The objects returned by this endpoint may be augmented with additional fields if localization is active. Fetch all of the global commands for your application. |
| **POST** | `/applications/{application.id}/commands` | Create a new global application command. |
| **GET** | `/applications/{application.id}/commands/{command.id}` | Get a specific global command. |
| **PATCH** | `/applications/{application.id}/commands/{command.id}` | Edit a specific global command. |
| **DELETE** | `/applications/{application.id}/commands/{command.id}` | Deletes a global command. Returns 204 No Content on success. |
| **PUT** | `/applications/{application.id}/commands` | Takes a list of application commands, overwriting the existing global command list for this application. Returns 200 and a list of application command objects. Commands that do not already exist will count toward daily application command create limits. This will overwrite all types of application commands: slash commands, user commands, and message commands. |

## Guild Application Commands

These endpoints manage commands scoped to a specific guild.

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| **GET** | `/applications/{application.id}/guilds/{guild.id}/commands` | The objects returned by this endpoint may be augmented with additional fields if localization is active. Fetch all of the guild commands for your application for a specific guild. Returns an array of application command objects. |
| **POST** | `/applications/{application.id}/guilds/{guild.id}/commands` | Create a new guild-scoped application command. |
| **GET** | `/applications/{application.id}/guilds/{guild.id}/commands/{command.id}` | Get a specific guild command. |
| **PATCH** | `/applications/{application.id}/guilds/{guild.id}/commands/{command.id}` | Edit a specific guild command. |
| **DELETE** | `/applications/{application.id}/guilds/{guild.id}/commands/{command.id}` | Delete a specific guild command. |
| **PUT** | `/applications/{application.id}/guilds/{guild.id}/commands` | Bulk overwrite all guild commands for a specific guild. |

## Key Notes

- Commands can only be registered via HTTP endpoint. Commands can be scoped either globally or to a specific guild. Global commands are available for every guild that adds your app.

- An individual app's global commands are also available in DMs if that app has a bot that shares a mutual guild with the user.

- Guild commands update instantly. We recommend you use guild commands for quick testing, and global commands when they're ready for public use.

- Application commands do not depend on a bot user in the guild; they use the interactions model. To create commands in a guild, your app must be authorized with the `applications.commands` scope which can be used independently, but is also automatically included with the bot scope.

- For authorization, all endpoints take either a bot token or client credentials token for your application.

- While most endpoints that return application command objects will return the `name_localizations` and `description_localizations` fields, some will not by default. This includes GET endpoints that return all of an application's guild or global commands. Instead, those endpoints will supply additional `name_localized` or `description_localized` fields, which only contain the localization relevant to the requester's locale. (The full dictionaries can still be obtained by supplying the appropriate query argument).

For full details, see the [official Discord documentation](https://discord.com/developers/docs/interactions/application-commands).

Here's a comprehensive list of the Discord REST API endpoints for **Roles**, **Prune**, **Integrations**, and **Widget** under the `/guilds/{guild.id}` resource:

---

## `/guilds/{guild.id}/roles` — Role Endpoints

`DELETE /guilds/{guild.id}/roles/{role.id}` — Delete a guild role. Requires the `MANAGE_ROLES` permission. Returns a 204 empty response on success. Fires a Guild Role Delete Gateway event. Supports the `X-Audit-Log-Reason` header.

From the endpoint definitions, the full set of role endpoints includes:
- **`GET /guilds/{guild.id}/roles`** — Get all guild roles.
- **`POST /guilds/{guild.id}/roles`** — Create a new guild role.
- **`PATCH /guilds/{guild.id}/roles`** — Modify guild role positions.
- **`PATCH /guilds/{guild.id}/roles/{role.id}`** — Modify a guild role.
- **`DELETE /guilds/{guild.id}/roles/{role.id}`** — Delete a guild role.

The full list of guild endpoints includes: Delete Guild Role, Get Guild Prune Count, Begin Guild Prune, Get Guild Voice Regions, Get Guild Invites, Get Guild Integrations, Delete Guild Integration, Get Guild Widget Settings, Modify Guild Widget, Get Guild Widget, Get Guild Vanity URL, Get Guild Widget Image, Get Guild Welcome Screen, Modify Guild Welcome Screen, Get Guild Onboarding, Modify Guild Onboarding, and Modify Guild Incident Actions.

---

## `/guilds/{guild.id}/prune` — Prune Endpoints

- **`GET /guilds/{guild.id}/prune`** — Returns an object with one `pruned` key indicating the number of members that would be removed in a prune operation. Requires the `MANAGE_GUILD` and `KICK_MEMBERS` permissions. By default, prune will not remove users with roles. You can optionally include specific roles via the `include_roles` parameter. Any inactive user that has a subset of the provided role(s) will be counted.

- **`POST /guilds/{guild.id}/prune`** — Begin a prune operation. For large guilds it's recommended to set the `compute_prune_count` option to `false`, forcing `pruned` to null. Fires multiple Guild Member Remove Gateway events. By default, prune will not remove users with roles. You can optionally include specific roles via the `include_roles` parameter. Any inactive user with a subset of the provided role(s) will be included. This endpoint supports the `X-Audit-Log-Reason` header.

---

## `/guilds/{guild.id}/integrations` — Integration Endpoints

- **`GET /guilds/{guild.id}/integrations`** — Returns a list of integration objects for the guild.

- **`DELETE /guilds/{guild.id}/integrations/{integration.id}`** — Delete the attached integration object for the guild. Deletes any associated webhooks and kicks the associated bot if there is one. Requires the `MANAGE_GUILD` permission. Returns a 204 empty response on success. Fires Guild Integrations Update and Integration Delete Gateway events. Supports the `X-Audit-Log-Reason` header.

> **Note:** The create, modify, and sync guild integration endpoints have been removed as bots have been blocked from using them.

---

## `/guilds/{guild.id}/widget` — Widget Endpoints

- **`GET /guilds/{guild.id}/widget`** — Returns a guild widget settings object. Requires the `MANAGE_GUILD` permission.

- **`PATCH /guilds/{guild.id}/widget`** — Modify a guild widget settings object for the guild. All attributes may be passed in with JSON and modified. Requires the `MANAGE_GUILD` permission. Returns the updated guild widget settings object. Fires a Guild Update Gateway event. Supports the `X-Audit-Log-Reason` header.

- **`GET /guilds/{guild.id}/widget.json`** — Returns the widget for the guild.

- **`GET /guilds/{guild.id}/widget.png`** — Returns a PNG image widget for the guild. Requires no permissions or authentication.

---

### Summary Table

| Endpoint Path | Methods | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `/guilds/{guild.id}/roles` | `GET`, `POST`, `PATCH` | List, create, reorder roles |
| `/guilds/{guild.id}/roles/{role.id}` | `PATCH`, `DELETE` | Modify or delete a role |
| `/guilds/{guild.id}/prune` | `GET`, `POST` | Get prune count / begin prune |
| `/guilds/{guild.id}/integrations` | `GET` | List integrations |
| `/guilds/{guild.id}/integrations/{integration.id}` | `DELETE` | Delete an integration |
| `/guilds/{guild.id}/widget` | `GET`, `PATCH` | Get/modify widget settings |
| `/guilds/{guild.id}/widget.json` | `GET` | Get widget data (public) |
| `/guilds/{guild.id}/widget.png` | `GET` | Get widget image (public) |

cd /workspace/scope-maps && python3 -c "
import json

for f in ['slack.json', 'discord.json']:
    with open(f) as fh:
        data = json.load(fh)
    print(f'{f}:')
    print(f'  provider: {data[\"provider\"]}')
    print(f'  apiHost: {data[\"apiHost\"]}')
    print(f'  basePath: {data[\"basePath\"]}')
    print(f'  scopeMap entries: {len(data[\"scopeMap\"])}')
    print(f'  allScopes entries: {len(data[\"allScopes\"])}')
    
    # Check for duplicate pathPattern+method combos
    seen = set()
    dupes = []
    for entry in data['scopeMap']:
        key = (entry['method'], entry['pathPattern'])
        if key in seen:
            dupes.append(key)
        seen.add(key)
    if dupes:
        print(f'  WARNING: {len(dupes)} duplicate method+pathPattern combos: {dupes[:5]}')
    else:
        print(f'  No duplicate entries')
    
    # Verify all scopes in scopeMap are in allScopes
    all_scopes = set(data['allScopes'])
    used_scopes = set()
    for entry in data['scopeMap']:
        for s in entry['sufficientScopes']:
            used_scopes.add(s)
    missing = used_scopes - all_scopes
    unused = all_scopes - used_scopes
    if missing:
        print(f'  WARNING: scopes used but not in allScopes: {missing}')
    if unused:
        print(f'  Note: scopes in allScopes but not referenced: {unused}')
    print()
"

The 5 Slack scopes that appear in `allScopes` but aren't referenced in `scopeMap` entries are supplementary/alternative scopes (like `chat:write.public` which extends `chat:write` to post in channels the bot hasn't joined, and `channels:write.invites` which is a more granular alternative to `channels:manage`). They are legitimately in the allScopes list.

Here is a summary of what was created:

---

## Files Created

### `/workspace/scope-maps/slack.json`

- **Provider:** Slack
- **API Host:** `slack.com`
- **Base Path:** `/api`
- **Endpoint Mappings:** 141 entries
- **Unique Scopes:** 61

Covers all major Slack Web API method families:

| Category | Methods Covered |
|----------|----------------|
| **chat.\*** | postMessage, update, delete, scheduleMessage, deleteScheduledMessage, scheduledMessages.list, meMessage, postEphemeral, unfurl, getPermalink |
| **conversations.\*** | list, info, history, replies, members, create, invite, kick, join, leave, archive, unarchive, rename, setPurpose, setTopic, open, close, mark, inviteShared, acceptSharedInvite, declineSharedInvite, listConnectInvites, externalInvitePermissions.set, canvases.create |
| **users.\*** | list, info, lookupByEmail, setPresence, getPresence, identity, conversations, profile.get, profile.set |
| **files.\*** | upload, uploadV2, list, info, delete, sharedPublicURL, revokePublicURL, completeUploadExternal, getUploadURLExternal, remote.add/info/list/remove/share/update |
| **reactions.\*** | add, remove, list, get |
| **pins.\*** | add, remove, list |
| **stars.\*** | add, remove, list |
| **search.\*** | messages, files, all |
| **reminders.\*** | add, delete, list, complete, info |
| **bookmarks.\*** | add, edit, list, remove |
| **team.\*** | info, accessLogs, billableInfo, integrationLogs, preferences.list, profile.get |
| **usergroups.\*** | create, disable, enable, list, update, users.list, users.update |
| **dnd.\*** | endDnd, endSnooze, info, setSnooze, teamInfo |
| **views.\*** | open, push, update, publish |
| **calls.\*** | add, end, info, update, participants.add, participants.remove |
| **Other** | auth.test, auth.revoke, auth.teams.list, api.test, bots.info, dialog.open, emoji.list, canvases.\*, apps.\*, oauth.\*, openid.\*, rtm.connect, metadata.message.read, workflows.\*, functions.\* |

All Slack API calls are POST requests. For `conversations.*` methods, multiple scopes are listed because the required scope depends on channel type (public channels use `channels:*`, private channels use `groups:*`, DMs use `im:*`, group DMs use `mpim:*`).

### `/workspace/scope-maps/discord.json`

- **Provider:** Discord
- **API Host:** `discord.com`
- **Base Path:** `/api/v10`
- **Endpoint Mappings:** 185 entries
- **Semantic Scopes:** 37

Covers all major Discord REST API v10 resources:

| Category | Endpoints Covered |
|----------|-------------------|
| **Guilds** | Get, create, modify, delete guild; preview; integrations; widget; vanity URL; welcome screen; onboarding; soundboard sounds; templates |
| **Channels** | Get, modify, delete; permissions; followers; typing; recipients; threads (create, join, leave, members, archived) |
| **Messages** | List, get, create, edit, delete; bulk delete; crosspost; pins |
| **Reactions** | Add own, remove own, remove others, list reactors, clear all |
| **Members** | Get, list, search, add, modify, kick |
| **Roles** | List, get, create, modify positions, modify, delete; add/remove role from member; member counts |
| **Bans** | List, get, ban, unban, bulk ban |
| **Webhooks** | Create, list (channel/guild), get, modify, delete, execute (including Slack/GitHub compat), message CRUD |
| **Voice** | Regions, voice states (get/modify self and others), stage instances |
| **Invites** | Get by code, delete, list (guild/channel), create |
| **Users** | Get current/other, modify, list guilds, guild member, leave guild, create DM, connections, role connections |
| **Audit Logs** | Get guild audit log |
| **Emojis** | List, get, create, modify, delete (guild and application) |
| **Stickers** | Get, list packs, guild stickers CRUD |
| **Scheduled Events** | List, create, get, modify, delete, get subscribers |
| **Auto-Moderation** | List rules, get, create, modify, delete |
| **Application Commands** | Global commands CRUD + bulk overwrite; guild commands CRUD + bulk overwrite; permissions |
| **Interactions** | Callback, original response, followup messages |
| **Applications** | Get/modify current app; role connection metadata |
| **Polls** | Get answer voters, expire poll |
| **Templates** | List, get, create, sync, modify, delete; create guild from template |

Path patterns use `*` as a wildcard for snowflake IDs (e.g., `/api/v10/guilds/*/channels`). The semantic scopes (e.g., `guilds.read`, `messages.write`, `members.read`) are designed for proxy-based policy enforcement and follow a `resource.action` convention.
